checks.title: Cached-route x middleware intersection
checks.detail: matcher pattern "/old-blog/:path*" (classification: rewrite, per-request) intersects the ISR route /blog/[slug] via its rewrite target - a CloudFront-cached response under /old-blog/* skips the middleware rewrite entirely, and cached /old-blog copies fork from /blog ISR revalidation. This pattern/route intersection alone drives the HIGH verdict. Matcher pattern "/dashboard/:path*" (classification: auth_gating) intersects only /dashboard, which is dynamic (rendered per-request, absent from prerender-manifest.json) and therefore NOT cacheable - it contributes nothing to this check's severity despite its auth_gating classification.
remediations: OpenNext external middleware option (Outcome A only)
CloudFront Functions for simple header/redirect logic (Outcome B)
checks.upgrade_input: upgrade to Next.js >= 16.2 for the Adapter API's typed build output (a confidence upgrade offer, never a migration prerequisite)

checks.title: ISR / on-demand revalidation completeness

severity_by_outcome.A: HIGH if the scaffold omits either the tag cache or the revalidation queue - /blog/[slug] is ISR (revalidate 3600), so the tag cache AND revalidation queue must be provisioned together, not just the incremental cache
severity_by_outcome.B: conditional - a single-container next start handles ISR in-process; HIGH only if autoscaling >1 task with ISR present and no shared cache strategy (scaling posture unknown at this point in the pipeline)

checks.detail: ISR detected via prerender-manifest.json (/blog/[slug], initialRevalidateSeconds 3600, 2 prerendered paths); both outcome severity rules computed now per severity_rule_by_outcome - report-render selects wording by the eventual recommendation
remediations: Outcome A: provision the tag cache AND revalidation queue together (scaffold-opennext.md)
Outcome B: add a shared cache handler for multi-instance deployments, or drop ISR in favor of cache-control headers + CloudFront invalidation
checks.upgrade_input: upgrade to Next.js >= 16.2 for the Adapter API's typed build output (a confidence upgrade offer, never a migration prerequisite)

checks.title: Build environment consistency
checks.detail: no direct detection - advisory check, always surfaced when Outcome A applies: local vs. CI OpenNext builds can chunk-mismatch, causing site-wide 500s
remediations: Deploy only from CI, never from a local OpenNext build

checks.title: Uncached high-invocation routes (cost driver flag)
checks.detail: /api/checkout is uncached (dynamic Route Handler, maxDuration 30), but invocation counts are unavailable - the usage API capture was skipped ('no usage aggregates exposed on hobby/pro plan without observability'), so the cache-config x invocation cross-reference cannot be completed; no dollar figure produced
remediations: Add appropriate caching (revalidate, Cache-Control headers) to the flagged route(s)
